title: google + amazon's anthropic markup — aninews ep 3
desc: Daily AI news satire. Half of Google's and Amazon's Q1 AI profit came from marking up their stake in Anthropic.

beats.name: press-conference-cold-open
beats.narration: On Thursday, Big Tech reported quarterly earnings.
beats.cinematography: Slow zoom-in on Pichai at podium (1.0× → 1.05×)

beats.name: alphabet-big-number-reveal
beats.narration: Alphabet posted sixty-two point six billion dollars in profit.
beats.cinematography: Slow zoom-out reveal of the floating $62.6B (1.05× → 1.0×)

beats.name: half-cracks-off
beats.narration: Roughly half of it came from marking up the value of a single investment.
beats.cinematography: Hard cut. Brief 0.1s magenta flash on the half-cracking moment

beats.name: palm-orb-reveal
beats.narration: An eight-billion-dollar bet, now carried on the books at seventy.
beats.cinematography: Punchy zoom-pop start on orb materializing (1.0× → 1.06× over 0.4s, hold)

beats.name: smash-cut-to-jassy
beats.narration: Amazon reported sixteen point eight billion dollars in pre-tax gains from the same company.
beats.cinematography: Smash-cut hard transition from beat 04. No fade.

beats.name: jassy-pie-chart-deadpan
beats.narration: Per Amazon's filing, that represented more than half of its quarterly pre-tax income.

beats.name: the-investment-is-anthropic
beats.narration: The investment, in both cases, is Anthropic.
beats.cinematography: Slow pan inward (mimics dolly-in toward the shared orb)

beats.name: series-g-mega-doc
beats.narration: Anthropic priced its latest round near a nine-hundred-billion-dollar valuation. The markups followed.

beats.name: willens-meltdown
beats.narration: Reports cite an accounting consultant noting that perhaps this wasn't the best idea the standards board ever came up with.
beats.cinematography: Subtle drift — let Willens's distress carry the beat, brief 0.1s magenta flash on the sweat-drop moment

beats.name: handshake-with-orb
beats.narration: The two largest stakeholders in Anthropic are Google and Amazon.
beats.cinematography: Slow zoom-in on the floating orb between their hands (1.0× → 1.04×)

beats.name: analyst-knowing-glance
beats.narration: Their best-performing AI product this quarter was each other's investment.

beats.name: hero-final-rooftop
beats.narration: Forty-five billion dollars in AI profits. Generated by owning a piece of an AI company.
beats.cinematography: Slow hero zoom-in on the rooftop figures (1.0× → 1.05× over full 6s)

sources.attribution: Half of Google's and Amazon's "blowout AI profits" came from a stake in Anthropic (Apr 30, 2026)
Alphabet Q1 2026 earnings release — $28.7B equity-value gain, ≈50% of $62.6B quarterly profit; $36.9B total equity gains
Amazon Q1 2026 earnings release — $16.8B pre-tax non-operating gains from Anthropic, >50% of pre-tax income; $8B → ~$70B unrealized stake value
Anthropic Weighs Funding Offers at Over $900 Billion Valuation (Apr 29, 2026)

transcript: On Thursday, Big Tech reported quarterly earnings. Alphabet posted sixty-two point six billion dollars in profit. Roughly half of it came from marking up the value of a single investment. An eight-billion-dollar bet, now carried on the books at seventy. Amazon reported sixteen point eight billion dollars in pre-tax gains from the same company. Per Amazon's filing, that represented more than half of its quarterly pre-tax income. The investment, in both cases, is Anthropic. Anthropic priced its latest round near a nine-hundred-billion-dollar valuation. The markups followed. Reports cite an accounting consultant noting that perhaps this wasn't the best idea the standards board ever came up with. The two largest stakeholders in Anthropic are Google and Amazon. Their best-performing AI product this quarter was each other's investment. Forty-five billion dollars in AI profits. Generated by owning a piece of an AI company.

writing.author: Claude
writing.role: script · beat plan · narration · sources audit
